description: All exterior siding, soffit, fascia, and porch elements are installed, inspected, and superintendent-approved before exterior painting begins.

categories.name: Pre-Install Verification
items: Housewrap (WRB) fully installed, lapped shingle-style top over bottom, all seams taped per manufacturer spec — no punctures, tears, or unbonded seams visible
Window and door rough openings confirm flashed with self-adhering flashing tape, sill pan sloped to exterior, and integrated with WRB before siding begins
Siding material confirmed on-site matches client-approved selection (species, profile, prefinished vs. field-painted, manufacturer, and SKU verified against Selections log)
Corner boards, frieze boards, and trim stock confirmed on-site in correct profiles and quantities per takeoff
All exterior blocking and nailer locations verified in framing — porch beam pockets, railing posts, ceiling fan blocking, outdoor TV blocking — before siding closes walls
Exterior electrical, gas, and hose bib penetrations stubbed and rough inspected by Baldwin County before housewrap and siding close those walls

categories.name: Siding Installation
items: First course of siding is level, starter strip set at correct height above foundation per manufacturer spec, and confirmed consistent around all four elevations

Siding nailed per manufacturer nailing schedule and IRC Table R703 wind-load requirements — nail type, penetration depth, and spacing verified (Baldwin County is HVHZ-adjacent; confirm 150 mph wind zone fastener schedule applies)
All horizontal laps consistent at each course — no variation greater than 1/8 inch across any 10-foot run, checked with story pole
All butt joints staggered minimum 24 inches between courses and landed on studs — no floating joints
Trim-to-siding and siding-to-trim joints back-primed on all cut ends before installation; caulk joints ≤ 3/8 inch and tooled smooth
All exterior penetrations (outlets, hose bibs, dryer vents, HVAC lines) properly flashed and sealed with compatible sealant before surrounding siding installed
J-channel or proper transition flashing used at all window and door perimeters; no exposed raw edges of siding material

categories.name: Soffit & Fascia
items: Fascia boards set straight, level, and in the same plane across each elevation — checked with string line; no deviation greater than 1/4 inch over 20 feet
Soffit panels installed per manufacturer spec — vented soffit panels at correct interval to meet minimum net free area required by IRC R806 for attic ventilation
Soffit-to-wall return and soffit-to-fascia joints tight and properly caulked; no gaps greater than 1/8 inch visible from ground
All soffit and fascia cut ends back-primed before installation
Can light and ceiling fan rough-in cans in porch ceilings verified plumb, secured to blocking, and trim rings fit flush before soffit panels close around them

categories.name: Porch Structure & Decking
items: Porch columns plumb to 1/8 inch per 8 feet in both directions; column bases set on proper flashing or standoff to prevent moisture wicking

Porch beams bearing fully on column capitals or ledger, bearing length meets structural engineer's specification, and any required post-to-beam hardware (Simpson or equivalent) installed and fully fastened

Porch decking (composite, tongue-and-groove, or hardwood per selection) installed with correct gap spacing for drainage and expansion — typically 1/8 inch per manufacturer; no cupped or bowed boards exceeding 3/16 inch over 6 feet
Porch ledger flashing fully integrated with WRB and housewrap behind siding — no exposed ledger-to-wall joint; verified watertight before adjacent siding installed
Porch ceiling (beadboard, T&G, or composite) installed per selection, joints running parallel to house front, consistent 1/16-inch reveal at each board, and all ends terminated at solid backing
Outdoor ceiling fan blocking and junction boxes verified load-rated (min. 35 lbs for fan, per NEC 314.27(C)) and accessible before porch ceiling closes

categories.name: Railings & Stairs
items: Porch railing system matches client-approved selection (material, profile, color) and confirmed installed before paint or stain phase
Railing post bases anchored to structural blocking or deck framing per engineer's spec — no surface-mount post bases on porch decking without structural approval
Baluster spacing confirmed ≤ 4 inches on center — no opening allowing passage of a 4-inch sphere, per IRC R312.1.3
Top rail height confirmed 36 inches minimum above deck surface for porches under 30 inches above grade; 42 inches for any elevated deck per IRC R312.1.1
Stair stringers bear fully on concrete or treated-lumber pad at base; riser and tread dimensions consistent within 3/8 inch across all treads per IRC R311.7.4
All pressure-treated lumber in ground-contact or porch stair locations confirmed rated UC4A or UC4B per AWPA; documentation on file

categories.name: Inspection & Sign-Off
items: Baldwin County exterior rough inspection (siding, WRB, and structural) passed and inspection card signed — copy scanned to project Drive folder
Superintendent visual final walk of all four elevations from ground level: no missing caulk joints, no nail pops, no misaligned courses, no unpainted back-primed cuts exposed
Client walkthrough of exterior and porch completed; client initials punch list (or confirms no items) before exterior paint phase is scheduled
Any open punch items from client walkthrough logged in Cost Tracker with responsible party and close-by date assigned
Siding sub's final invoice reviewed against contract scope; any material overages or change orders reconciled and CO signed before payment released
